Test Case Name: TC01_Verify Premium Calculation_With Additional Industry_Property_New Business_AC1
Description: Validate premium calculation considers primary and additional NAICS codes, applies percentage split per NAICS, rolls up coverage-peril premiums, and displays final premium in cost breakdown. Preconditions: Location with Primary + Additional Industry entries and percentage splits (e.g., 40/45/15). Applicable to Canada and International.
Action: 1. Create a new Property pricing request for a location with Primary NAICS and Additional Industry NAICS entries with percentage splits (e.g., Primary 40%, Additional1 45%, Additional2 15%).
Expected Result: 1. System accepts the location data and displays the NAICS codes and percentage splits.

Action: 2. Trigger rating/pricing for the policy/location.
Expected Result: 2. System calculates intermediate premiums for each NAICS code for each coverage-peril combination (e.g., PD-FLEXA) using corresponding industry inputs.

Action: 3. Verify percentage-split adjustment is applied to each NAICS premium.
Expected Result: 3. Each NAICS premium is multiplied by its percentage split/100 (Final Premium = Calculated Premium * Percentage / 100).

Action: 4. Verify roll-up of premiums into single premium per coverage-peril.
Expected Result: 4. System sums adjusted premiums across NAICS codes and reports a single premium for each Coverage-Peril combination.

Action: 5. Continue remaining rating calculations (blending, risk premium, term amount) on the rolled-up premium.
Expected Result: 5. System completes blending, risk premium, term amount calculations and displays final premium in the cost breakdown screen for that coverage-peril.

Action: 6. Repeat pricing for an International location with same NAICS setup.
Expected Result: 6. System performs the same calculations and displays the final premium for international locations.

Test Case Name: TC02_Verify Premium Calculation_With No Additional Industry_Property_New Business_AC2
Description: Validate premium calculation considers primary NAICS only when no additional industry is present. Preconditions: Location with Primary Industry only (no Additional Industry). Applicable to Canada and International.
Action: 1. Create a new Property pricing request for a location with only Primary NAICS and no Additional Industry entries.
Expected Result: 1. System accepts the location data and shows only the Primary NAICS.

Action: 2. Trigger rating/pricing for the policy/location.
Expected Result: 2. System calculates premium for each coverage-peril combination using Primary NAICS inputs only.

Action: 3. Verify that no additional NAICS calculations are performed.
Expected Result: 3. System does not perform separate calculations for additional NAICS and uses only primary NAICS results.

Action: 4. Verify final premium displayed in cost breakdown is based on primary NAICS calculations.
Expected Result: 4. Final premium in cost breakdown matches calculations derived from Primary NAICS only.

Action: 5. Repeat pricing for an International location with Primary-only setup.
Expected Result: 5. System performs the same Primary-only calculations for international locations.

Test Case Name: TC03_Verify Policy Change Pricing_Policy Bound Before Code Change_Property_Policy Change_AC3
Description: When pricing a policy change for a policy that was bound before this code change, ensure premium is calculated using primary industry only. Preconditions: Use a policy instance that was bound prior to the code change deployment.
Action: 1. Open an existing Property policy bound before the code change (set policy bound/effective date prior to deployment).
Expected Result: 1. System opens the policy with historical bound date and displays Primary and Additional Industry data as stored at bind time.

Action: 2. Initiate a policy change transaction that triggers re-pricing.
Expected Result: 2. System calculates pricing for the policy change transaction.

Action: 3. Verify that the pricing logic uses Primary NAICS only (ignores Additional Industry) for this bound-before policy.
Expected Result: 3. System uses Primary NAICS inputs only and does not apply additional NAICS-based repeats or percentage splitting.

Action: 4. Verify final premium displayed for the policy change reflects Primary-only calculations.
Expected Result: 4. Final premium shown for the policy change matches calculations based on Primary NAICS only.

Test Case Name: TC04_Verify Policy Change Pricing_Policy Bound After Code Change_Property_Policy Change_AC4
Description: When pricing a policy change for a policy that was bound after this code change, ensure premium is calculated considering both primary and additional industries. Preconditions: Use a policy instance bound after the code change deployment.
Action: 1. Open an existing Property policy bound after the code change (set policy bound/effective date after deployment) that includes Primary and Additional Industry entries with percentage splits.
Expected Result: 1. System opens the policy and displays Primary and Additional Industry entries and percentage splits.

Action: 2. Initiate a policy change transaction that triggers re-pricing.
Expected Result: 2. System calculates intermediate premiums for each NAICS code and coverage-peril combination.

Action: 3. Verify percentage-split adjustment is applied to each NAICS premium.
Expected Result: 3. Each NAICS premium is adjusted by its percentage split and then rolled up into a single premium per coverage-peril.

Action: 4. Verify final blended calculations and final premium display in cost breakdown for the policy change.
Expected Result: 4. System performs blending, risk premium, term amount calculations on the rolled-up premium and displays the final premium in the cost breakdown.

Action: 5. Confirm behavior for International locations bound after code change.
Expected Result: 5. System performs the same additional-industry-inclusive calculations for international locations bound after the change.
